name: Pre-flight checklist
action: Verify Node ≥ 20, `synaps rpc --help` returns 0, bridge.toml exists at ~/.synaps-cli/bridge/bridge.toml, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_APP_TOKEN are set, Slack app has required scopes and Socket Mode enabled, no other synaps-bridge process running.
expected: All checklist items pass without error.
rollback: Fix the failing item (install correct Node version, copy bridge.toml.example, export tokens, add Slack scopes, kill stray process) and re-verify.

name: Start daemon in foreground
expected: Log lines include 'synaps-bridge 0.1.0 starting', 'config loaded', 'slack adapter: connecting', 'control socket listening', and finally 'synaps-bridge ready'.
rollback: Check SLACK_BOT_TOKEN/SLACK_APP_TOKEN are exported. Verify synaps binary is on PATH. Confirm Socket Mode is enabled in Slack app settings and the app-level token has connections:write scope.

name: AI-app mode: basic prompt
action: Open a new AI-app thread in the Synaps Slack app DM, send 'hello'.
expected: Typing indicator appears via assistant.threads.setStatus. Streaming text reply arrives debounced (~80 chars / 250 ms). Reply completes, suggested prompts appear, thread title is set in sidebar.
rollback: If no typing indicator: verify assistant:write scope and Agents & AI Apps feature. If no reply: run --log-level debug and check for bot-gate blocked or synaps-rpc error.

name: Tool call rendering
action: In the same AI-app thread, send 'list my recent files'.
expected: A task_update card with status in_progress appears inside the stream (not as a separate message). Card updates to complete when the tool result arrives. No separate Block Kit bot messages.
rollback: If separate message: thread is in legacy mode — verify assistant_view is enabled. If card never completes: check synaps rpc stderr with --log-level debug.

name: Subagent lifecycle rendering
action: In the same AI-app thread, send 'spawn a research subagent to summarize today's news'.
expected: A task_update card cycles through pending → in_progress → complete with subagent name, task preview, and result preview. All inside the active stream; no separate bot messages.
rollback: If three separate messages instead of task cards: AI-app mode is off — re-enable Agents & AI Apps feature in Slack app settings. If card stays at pending: look for subagent_done in daemon debug logs.

name: Multi-thread isolation
action: Open a second AI-app thread. Send 'What was my first message to you?' in both Thread A and Thread B. Run: node scripts/control-cli.mjs threads
expected: control-cli shows two distinct rows with different thread keys. Thread A recalls 'hello'; Thread B has no memory of Thread A.
rollback: If only one row: both threads share a key — verify that thread_ts values differ between the two AI-app conversations. If cross-contamination: file a bug with both session keys.

name: Inline model switch
action: In Thread A, send a message whose first line is exactly 'set-model: claude-opus-4-7'. Then run: node scripts/control-cli.mjs threads
expected: Daemon logs show 'inline directive set-model=claude-opus-4-7' and 'response set_model ok=true'. The model column in control-cli output for Thread A shows claude-opus-4-7.
rollback: If model column unchanged: check that the directive is on the exact first line with no leading spaces. Enable --log-level debug and look for 'inline directive' log lines. If ok=false: verify the model ID string is valid for the installed synaps build.

name: Crash + restart resilience
action: Find the rpc child PID for Thread A (via control-cli threads --format=json or pgrep). Run: kill -9 <rpc_pid>. Then send a message in Thread A.
expected: Daemon logs warn about child exit (code 137), then log 'respawning with --continue <session_id>'. Reply arrives after brief delay. Model is still claude-opus-4-7.
rollback: If no respawn: session-router hit its retry limit; check for 'giving up' in logs. Delete the corrupt session file from ~/.synaps-cli/sessions/ and retry. If wrong model after respawn: known v0 limitation.

name: Legacy fallback (app_mention)
action: Temporarily disable Agents & AI Apps in the Slack app settings. Wait ~2 min. @-mention the bot in a public channel: '@Synaps hello from legacy mode'.
expected: Daemon logs show 'app_mention'. Bot replies in a thread on that message. Streaming is via chat.update edits (text accumulates). No typing indicator. Subagent events appear as separate Block Kit messages. Re-enable Agents & AI Apps after.
rollback: If bot does not reply: verify respond_to_mentions = true in bridge.toml and app_mentions:read scope is granted. If still seeing AI-app behaviour: wait longer for the workspace-level change to propagate.

name: Graceful shutdown and session persistence
action: Press Ctrl+C in the daemon terminal. Then check: cat ~/.synaps-cli/bridge/sessions.json
expected: Daemon logs 'Received SIGINT', stops all adapters, sends shutdown to rpc children, writes sessions.json, exits cleanly. sessions.json contains entries for both Thread A and Thread B.
rollback: If sessions.json is missing or empty: re-run with --log-level debug and look for session-store flush errors. If rpc children linger: kill them with pkill -f 'synaps rpc' and file a bug.
